description: Stage 2_1 frontier-aligned portfolio for parameter-golf. Rebased around the 2026-03-24 upstream scan: stronger 11L control, GPTQ-class deployment lane, XSA4 as a runnable proxy for XSA-all, and deferred curriculum plus TTT-protocol branches.

slots.name: shared_checkpoint_frontier_deploy_control
slots.why: Shared-checkpoint deployment control on a frontier-aligned trained checkpoint.
slots.validates: Late-stage attribution works on the stronger base.
slots.falsifies: Deployment-lane readouts are too unstable or unsupported to trust.
notes: This pack is intentionally centered on same-checkpoint attribution rather than retraining.

slots.name: shared_checkpoint_frontier_deploy_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for deployment-lane attribution on the same checkpoint.
slots.validates: Small deployment deltas can be interpreted.
slots.falsifies: The deployment lane is too noisy for fine ranking.
notes: Use before trusting GPTQ-lite-sized gains.

slots.name: shared_checkpoint_sliding_eval64
slots.why: Retains the strongest late-stage scoring lever, but now on top of a stronger base and deployment path.
slots.validates: Sliding eval still contributes meaningful score lift after the frontier base shift.
slots.falsifies: The remaining leaderboard gap is mostly elsewhere.
notes: Keep this checkpoint-matched. Do not mix with training changes in first readout.

slots.name: shared_checkpoint_doc_isolated_sliding
slots.why: Tests whether doc-aware boundaries stack with sliding on the stronger base.
slots.validates: Doc isolation adds real lift beyond plain sliding.
slots.falsifies: Continuous context is already the stronger policy for this setup.
notes: Treat as a child of sliding, not an independent first-wave claim.

slots.name: shared_checkpoint_gptq_lite_export
slots.why: Cheap export-side fallback if full GPTQ is delayed.
slots.validates: Even the lightweight clip-search path materially beats the current export.
slots.falsifies: Deployment gap is too large for half-measures.
notes: Use as the fast baseline for the GPTQ lane.

slots.name: shared_checkpoint_full_gptq_int6
slots.why: Highest-priority deployment technique in the current no-TTT frontier.
slots.validates: The export lane is truly first-order for us too.
slots.falsifies: Our current stack is bottlenecked somewhere else before GPTQ matters.
notes: This is the main deployment bet.

slots.name: shared_checkpoint_full_gptq_zstd22
slots.why: Tests whether stronger compression meaningfully improves the GPTQ deployment lane.
slots.validates: Bytes saved by zstd matter after GPTQ.
slots.falsifies: Compression backend is only secondary after GPTQ.
notes: Judge first on legality margin, then score.

slots.name: shared_checkpoint_full_gptq_fp16_embedding
slots.why: Tests whether the old embedding passthrough still matters after moving to the GPTQ lane.
slots.validates: Embedding fragility remains one of the main deployment bottlenecks.
slots.falsifies: GPTQ already solves most of that bottleneck.
notes: Useful only as a GPTQ child, not a standalone headline now.

slots.name: frontier_base_control_a
slots.why: Primary frontier-aligned training control. This replaces the older root-like baseline as the main anchor.
slots.validates: The stronger 11L/2048/3500 warmdown env base is stable enough for patch ranking.
slots.falsifies: All patch ranking is suspect because the control itself is still underpowered or unstable.
notes: This control already includes the stronger env profile. Patches are judged relative to this, not to the older 9L root recipe.

slots.name: frontier_base_control_b
slots.why: Noise floor for the revised frontier-aligned training screen.
slots.validates: Observed gains exceed rerun noise on the stronger base.
slots.falsifies: The new base is too noisy for subtle patch attribution.
notes: Use B0/B1 spread before trusting small helper gains.

slots.name: frontier_base_plus_muon_wd04
slots.why: Still one of the easiest frontier-aligned helpers and already supported in the local patch set.
slots.validates: MuonWD remains additive even after the stronger env base shift.
slots.falsifies: It was only compensating for the older weaker baseline.
notes: Treat as a helper candidate inside a stronger stack, not the lead story.

slots.name: frontier_base_plus_leakyrelu2
slots.why: One-line frontier patch with unusually strong cross-submission recurrence.
slots.validates: The activation really is one of the remaining first-order bottlenecks.
slots.falsifies: The effect was contingent on other missing stack pieces.
notes: Highest-priority pure training patch.

slots.name: frontier_base_plus_ema997
slots.why: EMA has become more frontier-typical than SWA-first smoothing.
slots.validates: EMA improves deployment quality on the stronger base.
slots.falsifies: EMA is only a tiny helper with no practical leverage for us.
notes: Judge on deployed score, not only on raw pre-quant curve.

slots.name: frontier_base_plus_xsa4
slots.why: XSA is now one of the strongest architecture/context signals in the no-TTT frontier.
slots.validates: Cross-sequence context is a remaining major gap in our base model.
slots.falsifies: The frontier dependence on XSA does not transfer cleanly here.
notes: Start with the last four layers, not XSA-everywhere.

slots.name: frontier_base_plus_gptq_lite_proxy
slots.why: Shared-checkpoint deployment attribution is not wired yet, so GPTQ-lite is screened here as a full-run export proxy.
slots.validates: Even the lightweight deployment lane already beats the current export path on a full run.
slots.falsifies: The deployment lane needs full GPTQ before it is visible, or training quality is the bigger bottleneck.
notes: Temporary B-pack proxy until the shared-checkpoint runner exists. VRL stays a deferred child.

slots.name: frontier_base_plus_partial_rope_lnscale
slots.why: Small-but-real refinement bundle copied from the merged SOTA pattern.
slots.validates: These low-cost helpers survive on top of the stronger base.
slots.falsifies: Their effect is too small to justify first-wave attention.
notes: This is intentionally a refinement bundle, not a mechanism-level purity test.

slots.name: promoted_frontier_parent
slots.why: Placeholder for the best promoted frontier-base survivor.
slots.validates: A clean parent exists for stack composition.
slots.falsifies: No single revised training patch was strong enough to promote.
notes: Fill from the best B-pack winner.

slots.name: promoted_frontier_parent_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for child-stack composition on the revised frontier base.
slots.validates: Composition gains exceed rerun noise.
slots.falsifies: The remaining signal is too small or too unstable.
notes: Only use after C0 exists.

slots.name: core_stack_muonwd_leakyrelu2
slots.why: Tests whether the easiest two frontier helpers stack cleanly.
slots.validates: Core gains are additive early.
slots.falsifies: The two mechanisms are mostly redundant.
notes: Populate only after the single-patch wins are real.

slots.name: core_stack_muonwd_ema
slots.why: Tests whether training-side regularization and smoothing complement each other.
slots.validates: MuonWD and EMA are additive rather than substitutive.
slots.falsifies: They are both solving the same deployment problem.
notes: Only after both parents survive.

slots.name: core_stack_muonwd_xsa4
slots.why: Tests whether the main optimizer helper stacks with the main context helper.
slots.validates: Their gains are distinct enough to matter together.
slots.falsifies: Only one of them is actually carrying signal.
notes: Useful bridge to the core no-TTT stack.

slots.name: frontier_core_nottt_stack
slots.why: Main no-TTT composition target for the revised portfolio.
slots.validates: The strongest revised single wins compose into a credible frontier core.
slots.falsifies: The frontier stack is more interaction-sensitive than expected.
notes: This is the main composition candidate.

slots.name: frontier_core_plus_best_deploy
slots.why: Tests the best no-TTT core stack with the best deployment winner attached.
slots.validates: The training and deployment wins are genuinely complementary.
slots.falsifies: One lane dominates and the stack is not worth the extra complexity.
notes: Populate using the Pack A winner.

slots.name: frontier_core_plus_best_arch_helper
slots.why: Spends remaining budget on the strongest surviving architecture helper such as VRL or VE128.
slots.validates: There is still meaningful headroom after the core stack is built.
slots.falsifies: The core stack already captures most of the available no-TTT gain.
notes: Prefer VRL first; VE128 only if deployment headroom exists.

slots.name: context_all_control_base
slots.why: Control for the XSA-all escalation pack.
slots.validates: The broader context pack has a stable no-TTT reference.
slots.falsifies: Context-pack readouts are too noisy to rank.

slots.name: context_all_control_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for the XSA-all escalation pack.
slots.validates: Small context deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: This pack is too noisy for fine ranking.

slots.name: xsa_all_solo
slots.why: Directly tests whether the frontier destination is broader XSA coverage rather than XSA4.
slots.validates: XSA-all has standalone signal on the stronger 11L base.
slots.falsifies: The XSA4 proxy already captures most of the available context gain.

slots.name: xsa_all_plus_partial_rope
slots.why: Checks whether broader XSA and partial RoPE are complementary rather than redundant.
slots.validates: The context lane still has geometry headroom.
slots.falsifies: XSA-all already captures the available long-context gain.

slots.name: xsa_all_plus_ln_scale
slots.why: Tests whether broader context wants extra norm discipline in deeper layers.
slots.validates: LN-scale helps the stronger context path stabilize.
slots.falsifies: The added scaling is just compensating for a weaker XSA4 regime.

slots.name: xsa_all_refinement_bundle
slots.why: Tests the modern context bundle rather than a single helper in isolation.
slots.validates: The current frontier context story is additive.
slots.falsifies: The bundle is mostly overfitting to older stacks.

slots.name: xsa_all_plus_muonwd
slots.why: Checks whether the main optimizer helper and broader context mechanism stack cleanly.
slots.validates: Context gain survives tighter matrix regularization.
slots.falsifies: One of the two is solving the same deployed-loss problem.

slots.name: xsa_all_plus_leakyrelu2
slots.why: Checks whether the strongest cheap training win stacks with XSA-all.
slots.validates: The context gain is not just compensating for weaker local features.
slots.falsifies: XSA-all mostly helps only on the unrefined baseline trunk.

slots.name: curriculum_control_base
slots.why: Control for shard-ordering screens under equal wallclock.
slots.validates: The curriculum pack has a stable reference.
slots.falsifies: Order-sensitive runs are too noisy to rank.

slots.name: curriculum_control_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for shard-ordering experiments.
slots.validates: Small equal-wallclock ordering deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: The curriculum lane needs more repeats before ranking.

slots.name: curriculum_reverse_order
slots.why: Cheap probe for whether simple order reversal matters under the time cap.
slots.validates: Data order is first-order enough to move the short-horizon endpoint.
slots.falsifies: Simple reorderings are mostly neutral on this base.

slots.name: curriculum_random_order
slots.why: Tests whether non-lexicographic exposure alone changes the 600-second endpoint.
slots.validates: The lane is worth a stronger externally ranked order.
slots.falsifies: Most of the reported gain depends on a specific quality-ranked schedule.

slots.name: curriculum_size_desc
slots.why: Uses file size as a cheap proxy ordering instead of an explicit external score file.
slots.validates: Crude information-ordering heuristics already move the endpoint.
slots.falsifies: The curriculum lane likely needs a smarter externally ranked order.

slots.name: curriculum_size_asc
slots.why: Counter-control to size-desc so we can see if order direction matters.
slots.validates: A directional schedule is doing something real rather than random drift.
slots.falsifies: File-size heuristics are just noise.

slots.name: curriculum_reverse_plus_ema
slots.why: Checks whether phase smoothing preserves any curriculum gain.
slots.validates: Ordering effects survive the train-to-export bridge.
slots.falsifies: The ordering win is too brittle to survive averaging.

slots.name: curriculum_size_desc_plus_leakyrelu2
slots.why: Checks whether a cheap training-shape win stacks with a crude curriculum heuristic.
slots.validates: Curriculum interacts constructively with improved local feature learning.
slots.falsifies: The curriculum effect is just a weak proxy for better optimization.

slots.name: geometry_control_base
slots.why: Control for deploy-quality geometry screens on the frontier-aligned base.
slots.validates: The geometry pack has a stable reference.
slots.falsifies: Deploy-oriented geometry effects are too noisy to rank.

slots.name: geometry_control_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for the geometry pack.
slots.validates: Small deploy-quality geometry deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: This pack needs more repeats before ranking.

slots.name: weight_perturbation
slots.why: Biases training toward flatter, more quantization-robust minima without changing the base architecture.
slots.validates: A cheap post-step noise process can improve deployed quality even when raw fp32 quality is unchanged.
slots.falsifies: The stage3 win was narrow to that setup and does not transfer to the stronger frontier base.
notes: Judge primarily on deployed metrics, not only pre-quant quality.

slots.name: gradient_centralization
slots.why: Removes the DC component of matrix gradients before the optimizer step, which may improve both training and deployed quality.
slots.validates: Gradient preprocessing is a real remaining optimizer surface on the frontier-aligned base.
slots.falsifies: The stage3 signal was only marginal and does not survive rebasing.

slots.name: late_weight_perturbation
slots.why: Tests whether noise helps most as a late flatness/compressibility bias rather than throughout training.
slots.validates: The weight-noise mechanism is phase-sensitive and should be applied only after useful features are formed.
slots.falsifies: The main gain comes from full-run noise rather than late-stage basin shaping.

slots.name: weight_perturbation_plus_ema
slots.why: Tests whether flatter minima and EMA-based export selection stack cleanly.
slots.validates: The flatness win survives and improves further after late smoothing.
slots.falsifies: EMA washes out the geometry win or both mechanisms solve the same thing.

slots.name: gradient_centralization_plus_ema
slots.why: Tests whether a small training-side geometry win stacks with deployment smoothing.
slots.validates: Gradient geometry and late export averaging are distinct enough to compose.
slots.falsifies: The centralization win is too small or too brittle to survive averaging.

slots.name: weight_perturbation_plus_grad_centralization
slots.why: Tests whether the two validated stage3 geometry mechanisms are additive on the stronger base.
slots.validates: Flatness bias and gradient preprocessing attack distinct optimizer surfaces.
slots.falsifies: One of the two is mostly proxying the other or the interaction is destabilizing.

slots.name: reference_base_control
slots.why: Reference control so the stage can be compared against a strong trunk and explicit SOTA-like anchors in one pack.
slots.validates: Reference comparisons are anchored to the same local baseline.
slots.falsifies: Reference pack noise makes anchor comparison unreliable.

slots.name: throughput_base_control
slots.why: Baseline for isolating whether direct FA3 calls buy useful step-time and score improvements.
slots.validates: The FA3 lane can be measured against an unchanged trunk.
slots.falsifies: FA3 results are too noisy to interpret against the current baseline.

slots.name: throughput_base_plus_fa3
slots.why: Directly tests whether FA3 is a free enough speedup to matter on the plain 11L trunk.
slots.validates: Kernel-only attention changes can buy useful extra steps under the wallclock cap.
slots.falsifies: FA3 is neutral or harmful even before stacking with heavier lanes.

slots.name: throughput_xsa_all_control
slots.why: Heavier attention/control stack without FA3, to isolate whether FA3 helps a broader-context lane.
slots.validates: The XSA-all lane is heavy enough that FA3 could matter.
slots.falsifies: The chosen context lane is too cheap for FA3 to move.

slots.name: throughput_xsa_all_plus_fa3
slots.why: Tests whether FA3 specifically helps a heavier XSA-all attention path.
slots.validates: FA3 is more useful once attention is the bottleneck.
slots.falsifies: FA3 does not materially help even a broader attention stack.

slots.name: throughput_frontier_lite_control
slots.why: Strong assembled no-TTT proxy without FA3, to check whether the kernel helps a near-frontier stack.
slots.validates: The frontier-lite stack is a meaningful base for throughput rebasing.
slots.falsifies: This proxy is too cheap or too noisy for FA3 to move.

slots.name: throughput_frontier_lite_plus_fa3
slots.why: Tests whether FA3 still helps once a strong local frontier-lite stack is already assembled.
slots.validates: Throughput gains survive rebasing onto a strong stack.
slots.falsifies: FA3 only helps weak trunks or its benefit disappears after rebasing.

slots.name: throughput_geometry_control
slots.why: Geometry-side winner without FA3, to see whether throughput helps a deploy-quality-biased lane.
slots.validates: The flatness lane still benefits from extra steps.
slots.falsifies: FA3 and geometry are mostly orthogonal in practice.

slots.name: throughput_geometry_plus_fa3
slots.why: Tests whether FA3 amplifies the geometry/compressibility gain by buying more effective updates.
slots.validates: The flatness win is partially compute-limited.
slots.falsifies: Geometry improvements are not bottlenecked by attention throughput.

slots.name: reference_base_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for the explicit reference pack.
slots.validates: Distance-to-anchor claims exceed rerun noise.
slots.falsifies: The reference pack needs more repeats before interpretation.

slots.name: merged_sota_proxy_a
slots.why: Best locally reproducible proxy for the merged SOTA recipe using currently implemented stage2_1 patches.
slots.validates: We can measure distance against a strong merged-SOTA-like stack, not only against the frontier trunk.
slots.falsifies: Even the merged-SOTA-like local proxy is not stable enough to use as an anchor.
notes: Proxy, not an exact reconstruction of merged PR #414.

slots.name: merged_sota_proxy_b
slots.why: Repeat of the merged-SOTA-like proxy for local noise estimation.
slots.validates: Distance-to-merged-proxy claims exceed anchor rerun noise.
slots.falsifies: The proxy itself is too unstable to serve as a useful bar.
notes: Proxy, not an exact reconstruction of merged PR #414.

slots.name: frontier_proxy_lite_a
slots.why: Best locally reproducible no-TTT frontier proxy in stage2_1 without the full GPTQ port.
slots.validates: We can compare new stage2_1 winners against a serious frontier-like assembled stack, not only isolated mutations.
slots.falsifies: The current local frontier proxy is too weak or unstable to serve as a distance estimate.
notes: Proxy only. Full frontier export quality still belongs in hailmary with full GPTQ.

slots.name: frontier_proxy_lite_b
slots.why: Repeat of the stage2_1 frontier-like proxy.
slots.validates: Distance-to-frontier-proxy claims exceed rerun noise.
slots.falsifies: The stage2_1 proxy is too noisy to be informative.
notes: Proxy only. Full frontier export quality still belongs in hailmary with full GPTQ.

slots.name: frontier_proxy_lite_plus_weight_perturbation
slots.why: Tests whether the new flatness/compressibility win improves a frontier-like local stack.
slots.validates: The geometry win still matters once a strong proxy stack is already present.
slots.falsifies: Weight perturbation only helps weaker trunks and does not move a strong assembled stack.

slots.name: frontier_proxy_lite_plus_grad_centralization
slots.why: Tests whether gradient geometry still helps once the stack is already close to the current frontier proxy.
slots.validates: Gradient preprocessing remains useful even on a strong assembled stack.
slots.falsifies: The centralization gain disappears once major frontier ingredients are present.

slots.name: tournament_control_a
slots.why: Primary frontier-aligned training control. This replaces the older root-like baseline as the main anchor.
slots.validates: The stronger 11L/2048/3500 warmdown env base is stable enough for patch ranking.
slots.falsifies: All patch ranking is suspect because the control itself is still underpowered or unstable.
notes: This control already includes the stronger env profile. Patches are judged relative to this, not to the older 9L root recipe.
synthetic_from=B0
stage=tournament_finalists

slots.name: tournament_control_b
slots.why: Noise floor for the revised frontier-aligned training screen.
slots.validates: Observed gains exceed rerun noise on the stronger base.
slots.falsifies: The new base is too noisy for subtle patch attribution.
notes: Use B0/B1 spread before trusting small helper gains.
synthetic_from=B1
stage=tournament_finalists

slots.name: winner_train_arch
slots.why: Small-but-real refinement bundle copied from the merged SOTA pattern.
slots.validates: These low-cost helpers survive on top of the stronger base.
slots.falsifies: Their effect is too small to justify first-wave attention.
notes: This is intentionally a refinement bundle, not a mechanism-level purity test.
synthetic_from=B7
winner_of=train_arch

slots.name: winner_context_all
slots.why: Tests whether broader context wants extra norm discipline in deeper layers.
slots.validates: LN-scale helps the stronger context path stabilize.
slots.falsifies: The added scaling is just compensating for a weaker XSA4 regime.
notes: synthetic_from=D4
winner_of=context_all

slots.name: winner_curriculum
slots.why: Uses file size as a cheap proxy ordering instead of an explicit external score file.
slots.validates: Crude information-ordering heuristics already move the endpoint.
slots.falsifies: The curriculum lane likely needs a smarter externally ranked order.
notes: synthetic_from=E4
winner_of=curriculum

slots.name: winner_geometry
slots.why: Biases training toward flatter, more quantization-robust minima without changing the base architecture.
slots.validates: A cheap post-step noise process can improve deployed quality even when raw fp32 quality is unchanged.
slots.falsifies: The stage3 win was narrow to that setup and does not transfer to the stronger frontier base.
notes: Judge primarily on deployed metrics, not only pre-quant quality.
synthetic_from=F2
winner_of=geometry

slots.name: winner_throughput
slots.why: Tests whether FA3 amplifies the geometry/compressibility gain by buying more effective updates.
slots.validates: The flatness win is partially compute-limited.
slots.falsifies: Geometry improvements are not bottlenecked by attention throughput.
notes: synthetic_from=U7
winner_of=throughput

slots.name: mix_train_context
slots.why: Tests whether the strongest train-side and broader-context survivors stack cleanly.
slots.validates: The training and context winners attack distinct bottlenecks and should compose.
slots.falsifies: The two strongest winners are mostly redundant on the 11L frontier base.
notes: synthetic_composite=B7+D4
